reviews.ownerResponse:
Jacob- I am truly as heartbroken as you are for your family. You were a valued client for well over a year and your case outcome, that was decided by a Judge after a trial, was the most difficult for me so far in my career. You and I both felt confident leaving court after our trial, and I hate that the outcome did not match our feelings that day.

Unfortunately, it is very common that not all evidence presented to an attorney by a client can be used in a trial. The main reason is evidence can be viewed in a light less favorable to my client, of which I would never want to submit to a Judge in the event it could back fire on my client. I only present evidence that, based on my education and experience, will give my client the best opportunity to reach his or her goal in trial. This is true for many aspects of the very complicated legal process.

I believe I used my education and training to the best of my ability to zealously advocate for you. At the end of the day, the judge makes the final decision, of which, like you, I did not agree with.

I was hopeful that an appellate attorney, of which area I do not specialize in, could assist you. I did not want to represent you in an appeal without the necessary experience thus, I referred you to wonderful colleagues in the area!

I hate to hear you were displeased with the services of my office as we seemed to have a wonderful attorney client relationship until this posting today. My office manager and I did our very best to support you and your family through the entire divorce process. Nevertheless, I will continue to pray for you and your boys as you weather this storm after a difficult and complex divorce. I wish you well in your future!

I had to, on multiple occasions, return to the office to sign documents that had already been signed or 'were forgotten' while I was there. I called and emailed with no responses. I lost an estate home sale because the documents weren't submitted to the court after 3 months of waiting- I had to call the probate court myself to check on this. When we got a secondary buyer of that property the exact same thing happened again but because I called the probate court myself we caught it in time to get it corrected. I ended up talking with the probate court on more than one occasion to check status because I couldn't obtain that information from my attorney or was told that we were waiting for the courts approval when in fact the documents hadn't even been submitted. I asked twice if I needed to seek council elsewhere and was told that it would be handled properly from there on out but it was not. Again, I know my case was a little different but as you can imagine it was a very hard time in my life and all of these issues didn't help in the slightest.
